Details: Bench- [2160, 2647], pilot [2659, 2668], and full-scale studies [2174, 2175, 2441, 2508, 2509, 2518, 2619, 2645] found conventional ozonation ineffective for removal of per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFASs). A demonstration study of a patented ozofractionation process, which used ozone gas to separate PFASs into a foam residual, found greater than 98 percent removal of total PFAS, including greater than 97 percent removal of perfluorooctanoic acid (PFOA), greater than 82 percent removal of perfluoropentanoic acid (PFPeA), greater than 96 percent removal of perfluorohexanoic acid (PFHxA), and greater than 99 percent removal of fluorotelomer sulfonate 6:2 (FtS 6:2) [2573].
